title: Transcript of the First Moon Landing - American Philosophical Society
content: Astronauts on the first mission to the moon were mission commander Neil Armstrong and pilot Buzz Aldrin. Armstrong was the first person to walk on the moon.

content: Apollo 11 astronauts Neil Armstrong, Michael Collins, and Buzz Aldrin pose in white NASA spacesuits with the Moon in the background. Astronaut stands on the moon with a large bootprint in the foreground and bold text reading Interactive: A Tour of the Moon's Landmarks. Neil Armstrong Takes Humankind's First Steps on the Lunar Surface During NASA's Apollo 11 Mission  footprint on the Moon   Buzz Aldrin on the Moon   Apollo 10. Apollo 11 astronaut Edwin Aldrin, photographed July 20, 1969, during the first manned mission to the Moon's surface. Aldrin (Buzz Aldrin) stands on the moon, Apollo 11. Buzz Aldrin on the Moon U.S. astronaut Buzz Aldrin walking on the Moon, July 20, 1969. The lunar module *Eagle* of the Apollo 11 mission landed on the Moon on July 20, 1969. **Apollo 11**, U.S. spaceflight during which commander Neil Armstrong and lunar module pilot Edwin (“Buzz”) Aldrin, Jr., on July 20, 1969, became the first people to land on the Moon and walk the lunar surface.

title: The 1st footsteps on the moon: Celebrate with Moon Day
content: # The 1st footsteps on the moon: Celebrate with Moon Day. Fuzzy black and white photo of an astronaut stepping off ladder of the lunar lander. The world watched on television as Neil Armstrong from Apollo 11 was the 1st human to leave footsteps on the moon on July 20, 1969. *Read all about the 1st footsteps on the moon – 56 years ago – and celebrate International Moon Day.*. On July 20, 1969, Apollo 11 astronauts Buzz Aldrin and Neil Armstrong landed their lunar module on a broad dark lunar lava flow, called the Sea of Tranquility. In the video below, you can hear the excitement in Armstrong’s voice at the successful landing of Eagle on the moon’s surface as he says:. And then they blasted off in their module from the lunar surface to meet up with Michael Collins in the command module orbiting overhead. Here is the Apollo 11 lunar module, the vehicle that would carry Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin to the moon’s surface.
